title: Set up Progressive Web App (PWA) in Next.js
summary: Add PWA support to a Next.js app with a web manifest, service worker caching, and install prompt using @ducanh2912/next-pwa.
declaration: I will add Progressive Web App (PWA) support to this Next.js project using @ducanh2912/next-pwa. This makes your app installable on mobile and desktop and enables offline caching via a service worker. The original next-pwa package is abandoned — I will use the actively maintained fork. If you need a fully custom service worker (push notifications, background sync), say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Set up PWA in Next.js ### 1. Install the package - Install the maintained fork (the original `next-pwa` is abandoned and broken on Next.js 13+): ``` npm install @ducanh2912/next-pwa ``` - Do NOT install `next-pwa` — it is unmaintained and incompatible with App Router. ### 2. Configure `next.config.ts` - Wrap your Next.js config with `withPWA`: ```ts import withPWA from '@ducanh2912/next-pwa'; import type { NextConfig } from 'next'; const nextConfig: NextConfig = {}; export default withPWA({ dest: 'public', // service worker output directory cacheOnFrontEndNav: true, // cache pages during client-side navigation aggressiveFrontEndNavCaching: true, reloadOnOnline: true, // auto-reload when coming back online disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development', // no SW in dev })(nextConfig); ``` - Setting `disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'` prevents service worker interference during development. - The generated `sw.js` and `workbox-*.js` files land in `public/`. Add them to `.gitignore`: ``` # PWA generated files public/sw.js public/workbox-*.js public/sw.js.map public/workbox-*.js.map ``` ### 3. Create the Web App Manifest (`public/manifest.json`) - Create `public/manifest.json`: ```json { "name": "My App", "short_name": "MyApp", "description": "My Next.js Progressive Web App", "start_url": "/", "display": "standalone", "background_color": "#ffffff", "theme_color": "#000000", "icons": [ { "src": "/icons/icon-192x192.png", "sizes": "192x192", "type": "image/png" }, { "src": "/icons/icon-512x512.png", "sizes": "512x512", "type": "image/png", "purpose": "any maskable" } ] } ``` - Replace `name`, `short_name`, `description`, `theme_color`, and `background_color` with your actual app values. - `display: "standalone"` hides the browser UI when installed — use `"minimal-ui"` if you want to keep navigation controls. ### 4. Add icons - Create the `public/icons/` directory. - Minimum required icons: `192x192` and `512x512` PNG files. - Use a tool like https://realfavicongenerator.net or generate from an SVG: ``` npx sharp-cli --input ./public/icon.png --output ./public/icons/icon-192x192.png resize 192 192 npx sharp-cli --input ./public/icon.png --output ./public/icons/icon-512x512.png resize 512 512 ``` - The `maskable` icon (512×512 with `purpose: "any maskable"`) requires safe-zone padding (about 10%). Use https://maskable.app to check. ### 5. Add manifest and theme-color meta tags to the root layout - Update `app/layout.tsx`: ```tsx import type { Metadata } from 'next'; export const metadata: Metadata = { manifest: '/manifest.json', themeColor: '#000000', appleWebApp: { capable: true, statusBarStyle: 'default', title: 'My App', }, formatDetection: { telephone: false, }, }; export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( {children} ); } ``` - Next.js 13+ handles `` and `` automatically from the `metadata` export. ### 6. Add iOS-specific meta tags (optional but recommended) - Add to `metadata` in `layout.tsx` for proper iOS home screen behavior: ```tsx export const metadata: Metadata = { // ...existing fields... icons: { apple: '/icons/icon-180x180.png', // iOS home screen icon }, }; ``` - Create a `180x180` PNG icon for iOS. - iOS uses its own icon system and ignores the manifest icons — a separate `apple-touch-icon` is required. ### 7. (Optional) Custom offline fallback page - Create `app/offline/page.tsx`: ```tsx export default function OfflinePage() { return (

You are offline

Check your internet connection and try again.

); } ``` - Register it in `next.config.ts`: ```ts export default withPWA({ dest: 'public', disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development', fallbacks: { document: '/offline', // served when a page is unavailable offline }, })(nextConfig); ``` ### 8. Verify the PWA - Run `npm run build && npm start` (service worker only runs in production mode). - Open Chrome DevTools → Application → Service Workers — verify `sw.js` is registered. - Application → Manifest — verify all manifest fields appear correctly. - Application → Cache Storage — confirm routes are cached by Workbox. - Run Lighthouse (DevTools → Lighthouse tab) → select PWA — aim for green checkmarks on all PWA criteria. - On Chrome: an install prompt ("Add to Home Screen") should appear in the address bar after a moment. ### Common errors & fixes - **Service worker not updating** → Hard-refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) or go to DevTools → Application → Service Workers → click "Update". - **`next-pwa` import fails** → You installed the abandoned package. Uninstall it and install `@ducanh2912/next-pwa` instead. - **Manifest not found (404)** → You created `manifest.json` in the wrong directory. It must be in `public/manifest.json`. - **Icons not loading** → Check paths in `manifest.json` match the actual filenames in `public/icons/`. - **`Cannot find module 'workbox-*'`** → Run `npm install` — `@ducanh2912/next-pwa` includes Workbox as a dependency. - **Service worker logs show MISS for all requests in dev** → Normal — service worker is disabled in development (`disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'`). - **Install prompt not appearing** → Must be served over HTTPS (or localhost), have a valid manifest, and have a service worker registered. Verify all three. - **iOS: app opens in Safari instead of standalone** → iOS requires the `apple-mobile-web-app-capable` meta tag. Set `appleWebApp: { capable: true }` in `metadata`. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT install the original `next-pwa` package — it is unmaintained and broken with Next.js 13+ App Router. Use `@ducanh2912/next-pwa`. - Do NOT enable the service worker in development (`disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'` should always be set). Service workers in dev cause confusing caching bugs. - Do NOT commit the generated `sw.js` and `workbox-*.js` files — add them to `.gitignore` as they are build artifacts. - Do NOT skip the `maskable` icon purpose — without it, installed app icons appear in a white box on Android. - Do NOT use `display: "fullscreen"` unless your app genuinely fills the whole screen — it hides browser back/forward and confuses users. - Do NOT assume service workers work in private/incognito mode — browsers disable service workers there. - Do NOT fetch API data in service workers without careful cache invalidation strategy — stale data served from cache is hard to debug.
